file.content:
# Review: deepmind/75.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 75`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/75](https://www.erdosproblems.com/75)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (existence of an ÔäÁÔéü-chromatic graph with large independent sets in every vertex subset).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[EHS82]`, `[Er95]`, `[Er95d]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos75 ... end Erdos75`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos75`.
- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block: `open SimpleGraph Cardinal` on line 36.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A ÔÇö No helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_75` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib conventions: `erdos_75`, `erdos_75.variants.linear`.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line** (e.g., `@[category research open, AMS 5]`).
- [x] Both theorems have docstrings (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Theorem docstrings reference source papers: `[EHS82, p. 120][Er95, p. 11][Er95d, p. 63]`.

### `answer()` usage

The English problem asks "Does there exist a graph...?" ÔÇö this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) Ôćö P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the **question as originally asked**: existence of a graph on ÔäÁÔéü vertices with the specified chromatic and independence-number properties.
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)` (inside the `Ôćö`, not as theorem parameters).
- [x] The variant `erdos_75.variants.linear` also correctly uses `answer(sorry) Ôćö P` form (same yes/no structure).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] The value is `research open` ÔÇö valid.
- [x] Both `research open` theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order (single number, trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjectures use `by sorry` (multi-line form: `:= by\n  sorry`, which is acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations (N/A ÔÇö none present).
- [x] No `sorry` in any file under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/` (N/A ÔÇö not in that directory).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant is named `erdos_75.variants.linear` (correct naming convention).
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research open, AMS 5]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring describing it as a "linear variant" with `c > 0` constant factor.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `Ôłâ`, `ÔłÇ`, `╬Á`, `ÔäŁ`, `ÔäĽ`, `Ôëą`, `ÔŐć`, `┬Č`, `Ôćö`, `Ôćĺ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all well under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` ÔÇö not verified in this review (requires build environment).
- [ ] All five custom linters pass ÔÇö not verified in this review (requires build environment).

---

**Summary:** All checkable items pass. The file is well-structured with correct copyright, import, module docstring with references, proper namespace, correct `answer()` usage for a yes/no existence question (Case 1), valid category and AMS attributes, and a cleanly named variant. No issues found.

content.text: The review passed ÔÇö all checkable items pass with no issues found. No changes needed.
